description:
# Why

bl-7523 made `bl review` (local-squash) and `bl close` (deferred /
`--delivered <sha>`) auto-tag `delivered_repo` with the current
clone's origin URL. That's the right default — the operator usually
runs the command from the clone that produced the sha — but it
silently lies when the operator explicitly points at a sha that lives
elsewhere.

Concrete case: the bridge clone in a multi-repo project (the proxy
pattern, README §Bridging to an external tracker) is where forge
plugins run sync. A forge merge produces a sha on client A's main;
the bridge clone (often a *different* code repo, or no code at all)
runs `bl close --delivered <sha>` from a sync hook. Today
`delivered_repo` gets set to the bridge's origin, not client A's.
The sha is then advertised against the wrong repo.

# Scope

- Add `--delivered-repo <url>` to `bl close`. When set, it overrides
  the auto-tagged provenance the same way `--delivered <sha>`
  overrides the tag scan.
- Behavior: pure parameter pair. `--delivered-repo` without
  `--delivered` is a no-op for the sha and only writes the
  provenance; `--delivered` without `--delivered-repo` keeps current
  behavior (use the local clone's origin). Both together fully
  describe a cross-repo delivery.
- Plumb through `delivery::populate_on_close` — same signature
  extension as the existing `manual: Option<String>` arg.

# Why now

The bl-7523 maintainer pass flagged this as a real footgun: the
local-as-default is fine, but an operator should always be able to
tell the tool the truth. The fix is small (one new optional flag,
one threaded arg) and bounds the surface area of bl-7523's
auto-tagging.

# Out of scope

- `bl review --delivered-repo`: review's provenance comes from the
  current clone by construction (the squash happens here). Not the
  same case.
- A `clients` registry mapping short tags → URLs (Option 2 from
  bl-7523's design): independent feature; file separately if
  warranted.

# Origin

Footgun #3 from the bl-7523 review pass.
title: bl close --delivered-repo: operator override for delivery provenance
